discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tape: SPY -1.58%, QQQ -2.0%, VIX +11.8%, semis hit hardest (SMH -3.4%) on what appears to be a CPI hot print (4.2%) plus escalating Iran rhetoric. Energy and staples are the only green sectors. Most mid-cap movers today are sympathy victims of the broad sell-off, but Masimo stands out with a multi-item 8-K signaling a major corporate transaction despite a flat tape.

notable_index_moves: SMH -3.4% leading tech down; AVGO -5.1%, TSM -4.5%
XLE +1.5% and XLP +1.65% the only green sectors — classic defensive/oil bid on Iran escalation + hot CPI

discoveries.name: Masimo Corporation
discoveries.setup: Multi-item 8-K signaling a major corporate transaction with stock flat on 3x volume
discoveries.thesis: The 8-K bundles Item 1.02 (terminated agreement), 2.01 (completed disposition), 3.01 (delisting/listing rule), 3.03 (security holder rights modification), 5.01 (change in control), 5.02 (officer/director change), and 5.03 (charter amendment). That combination is the fingerprint of a closed spin-off or divestiture of the consumer/Sound United business — long anticipated by activists. Stock flat on triple volume suggests the market views the transaction terms as in-line with what was already priced in. Magnitude (zero move) is rational given the event was telegraphed.
discoveries.what_confirms: Press release confirming Sound United divestiture closing, with cash/equity terms matching prior guidance; activist (Politan) commentary signaling satisfaction.
discoveries.what_kills: If the 8-K reveals an unexpected dilutive financing, a surprise CEO/leadership shake-up, or terms materially worse than expected, the flat tape becomes the mispricing and downside opens up.
discoveries.what_to_learn: 8-K bundles containing 2.01 + 3.03 + 5.01 + 5.03 together almost always signal a closed structural transaction (spin/merger/divestiture); flat stock reaction on heavy volume usually means the deal was pre-telegraphed and terms met expectations.
discoveries.catalyst: Multi-item 8-K consistent with closing of a previously-telegraphed divestiture or spin-off
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: The seven-item 8-K (1.02, 2.01, 3.01, 3.03, 5.01, 5.02, 5.03) is the textbook signature of a closing corporate transaction, not bad news bundling.
research_pointers: Read the actual 8-K exhibits — confirm whether this is the Sound United divestiture closing
Check Politan Capital's latest 13D filing for activist response
Compare divestiture terms to street expectations from H1 2026 sell-side notes
Look at Masimo's healthcare/core business pro-forma margins post-divestiture

discoveries.name: Insmed Incorporated
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap biotech down sharply with no 8-K or news catalyst visible
discoveries.thesis: No filings, no news, modest 1.3x volume on a -7% day with XBI -1.9%. Move is roughly 4x the sector beta but I can't articulate a specific bull or bear thesis without knowing the cause. Could be brensocatib commercial concern, a clinical leak, or a single fund unwind.
discoveries.what_confirms: Any leaked data on brensocatib launch trajectory, an analyst downgrade, or a competitor readout that would explain the magnitude.
discoveries.what_kills: If a clean explanation appears (broker conference comment, secondary offering rumor), the call should be re-evaluated immediately.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filing or news in our feed
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; INSM moved roughly 4x sector beta without explanation.
research_pointers: Check biotech conference calendars (ASCO afterglow, ADA, etc.) for any brensocatib commentary
Scan StockTwits/Twitter for any rumor flow
Check options flow for unusual put activity preceding the move

discoveries.name: Acuity Inc.
discoveries.setup: Industrial mid-cap down hard with no company-specific catalyst on a broad industrials sell-off
discoveries.thesis: XLI -3.4% today, AYI -6.5% with no 8-K and volume only 1.07x average. That's roughly 2x sector beta on an industrial that's now down ~10% over five days. The lack of any volume confirmation or filing suggests pure sympathy/multiple compression from the CPI-driven rate-up move, not company-specific deterioration. Lighting/electrical demand is cyclical but Acuity's quality and balance sheet argue against the magnitude.
discoveries.what_confirms: No 8-K appearing in next 48 hours and tape stabilizing — supports a sympathy fade thesis.
discoveries.what_kills: An after-hours 8-K appearing tonight or tomorrow (especially 2.05 restructuring or guidance withdrawal); a competitor pre-announcement on commercial construction weakness.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Quality industrial mid-caps with no filing trail and only modest volume multiple on a 2x-sector-beta down move are reliable mean-reversion candidates over days-to-weeks horizons.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with industrials sector (XLI -3.4%) on hot CPI + Iran headlines, no company-specific news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + 1.07x volume + 2x sector beta drop is the classic sympathy-overshoot fingerprint.
research_pointers: Confirm no 8-K hits over next 48 hours
Check Acuity's exposure to commercial construction vs. residential — sensitivity to rates
Compare to peers (HUBB, NVT) to confirm sector-wide nature

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Airline name down hard on Iran/oil escalation with no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: ALK -7.6% with NO volume confirmation (0.96x avg) and no 8-K. Crude is bid on Iran strikes (XLE +1.5%) and that's the obvious driver — jet fuel is the largest variable cost. But the magnitude is excessive for a one-day oil move, and ALK's hedge book + domestic-heavy route mix make it less exposed than international carriers. Sub-average volume suggests passive/algo selling rather than informed dumping.
discoveries.what_confirms: Crude reverses on any Iran de-escalation headline; no 8-K appears; peer airlines (DAL, UAL) show similar overshoot pattern.
discoveries.what_kills: Sustained crude breakout above key resistance; ALK 8-K with guidance cut; specific Alaska/Hawaiian integration negative news.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Airlines down 7%+ on oil-spike days WITHOUT volume confirmation are typical 1-3 day bounce candidates if the geopolitical catalyst is rhetoric rather than supply disruption.
discoveries.catalyst: Oil/Iran sympathy — jet fuel cost concern on Trump Iran strike rhetoric
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + sub-average volume on a -7.6% day during an oil spike is consistent with sympathy overshoot, not fundamental news.
research_pointers: Check ALK's current jet fuel hedge percentage and average hedge price
Compare ALK's drop to DAL/UAL/LUV today — sector vs. name-specific
Monitor Brent crude reaction to next Iran headline cycle

discoveries.name: WEX Inc.
discoveries.setup: Fleet payments name down sharply on light volume with no catalyst
discoveries.thesis: WEX -7.4% on essentially average volume (1.02x), no 8-K, no news. WEX has dual exposure to fleet fuel cards (oil sensitivity, but generally positive when prices rise via spread) and corporate travel payments. Direction of oil sensitivity is ambiguous — historically WEX has been a beneficiary of higher fuel prices, not a victim. Can't construct a clean conf-3 thesis either direction.
discoveries.what_confirms: News of a customer loss, a regulatory headline on interchange, or a guidance pre-announcement.
discoveries.what_kills: Tape stabilizes and stock recovers — suggests illiquid mid-cap algo overshoot.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; magnitude not explained by any visible driver.
research_pointers: Check for any analyst day or conference appearance in last 24h
Monitor interchange/Durbin regulatory headlines
Check options flow for unusual activity

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Hot CPI (4.2%) + Trump pledges renewed Iran strikes — risk-off + oil bid
Oracle reports beat but plans $20B raise — stock down after-hours

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump claims successful Strait of Hormuz naval blockade and ongoing pressure on Iran; combined with separate 'attacking them very hard' messaging
trump_signals.note: Mixed signal — Trump simultaneously claims oil is flowing AND threatens more strikes. Net market read is risk-off + oil bid as traders weight the escalation rhetoric more than the reassurance. Defense names should benefit; airlines suffer near-term.

ai_announcements.headline: Google cuts price on budget AI subscription tier, opening price war
ai_announcements.summary: Google reduced pricing on its budget AI consumer subscription, escalating competition with OpenAI/Anthropic/Perplexity on consumer AI pricing.

ai_announcements.headline: Cybersecurity researchers complain Anthropic Fable guardrails are too strict
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic's new Fable model has guardrails so strict that cybersecurity researchers say it's unusable for legitimate cyber work. Neutral-to-slightly-negative for Anthropic share in cyber-tooling market; modestly positive for incumbent cyber vendors and competing models.

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks to fund AI capex
ai_announcements.summary: Amazon stacking debt on top of recent bond sale to fund AI buildout. Signals continued hyperscaler capex but raises balance sheet concerns.

ai_announcements.headline: Meta signs first India AI data center deal with Reliance (168MW)
ai_announcements.summary: Continued hyperscaler datacenter expansion. Bullish read-through for power/electrical equipment suppliers and semicap.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Acuity (lighting/electrical) is arguably a beneficiary of datacenter buildout, not a victim of AI. -6.5% today and -10% over 5 days is unlikely AI-news driven; more likely earnings/guidance or sector rotation.
research_pointers: Check AYI earnings calendar - they typically report late June/early July; today's move smells like a pre-announce or analyst downgrade
Confirm no AYI-specific 8-K or guidance cut
If move is purely sector/macro, datacenter electrical exposure thesis intact

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Entegris (semicap materials/filtration) benefits from AI chip buildout volume. -4% today / -8% week likely tied to broader semicap weakness or China export concerns, not AI-negative news. Counter-thesis intact.
research_pointers: Check for semicap peer moves (LRCX, AMAT, KLAC) today
Any China export restriction headlines this week
ENTG has been a consensus AI infrastructure long - watch for capitulation

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The biggest decliners today (INSM -7.2%, ALK -7.6%, WEX -7.4%, ALGN -4.3%, MASI flat on heavy volume w/ multi-item 8-K) have no plausible AI-news linkage. INSM is biotech (likely trial/data), ALK is airlines (fuel/guide), WEX is fleet payments (likely guide cut), MASI's 8-K with delisting/officer departure/acquisition items is the actual story there. None should be analyzed under AI-overreaction framework. AYI and ENTG flagged speculatively as possible AI-capex beneficiaries being mis-sold, but moves are more likely company/sector specific - low confidence.
